# Chapter One - The Dark Forest

Roland Deschain walked east through the dark forest, his revolvers heavy at his hips. The boy Jake followed three paces behind, saying nothing.

The trees here were ancient, their bark black and smooth as oil. No birds sang. The ka-tet had been walking for three days when they found the first sign: a doorway carved into a living tree, its edges pulsing with faint blue light.

"What is it?" Jake whispered.

"A door," Roland said. "One of the magical doorways that connect worlds."

Jake reached out to touch the pulsing light. "Should we go through?"

"Not yet," Roland said. "We must understand what lies beyond before we step through."

The magic in this world was old — older than Roland, older than the gunslingers, older than the Affiliation itself. It moved through the land like blood through veins.
